      "title": "Legacy write failures silently ignored in Create operation",
      "description": "In the Create method, errors from d.Legacy.Create() are logged but not returned to the caller. This means the operation succeeds to the client even though the legacy storage write failed, creating a data consistency gap between Storage and LegacyStorage. Since Mode 3 is supposed to write to both systems, a silent failure on one side violates the dual-write contract.",
      "suggestion": "Decide on the failure semantics: either return an error if legacy write fails (fail-safe), or at minimum return a wrapped error that indicates partial success. For a dual-write system, both writes should succeed or the entire operation should fail."

      "severity": "medium",

      "description": "The diff shows `apierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"` being removed and replaced with generic `\"errors\"`. If the code previously used apierrors.IsAlreadyExists(), apierrors.IsNotFound(), or other Kubernetes-specific error predicates, replacing with generic errors will break error classification logic. The truncated diff prevents full verification, but this is a risky change for Kubernetes API code.",
      "suggestion": "Verify that all error handling logic still uses appropriate Kubernetes error predicates. If apierrors was used for error type checking, restore those checks or update them appropriately."
